ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08118New York Revolutionary Groups collection8118Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
November 20172B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
rghisThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:08:35 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
New York Revolutionary Groups collection
Kugelberg, Johan
8118
1.9 cubic feet. 1.9 cubic feet.
1966-1971. Printed material, publications, photocopies, newspapers. Materials documenting influential radical groups of the 1960s. The collection includes a complete run of Black Mask and 61 issues of Rat Subterranean News, 90 flyers and handbills relating to the New York anarchist groups Black Mask and Up Against the Wall Motherfuckers, as well as related affinity groups and others such as the Yippies, ESSO (The East Side Service Organization, or the East Side Survival Organization), and New York Provo. Collection material in English 9756210
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
November 2017
EAD encoding:
Jude Corina, November 2017
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Black Mask was a radical group and newspaper created in 1966 by abstract painter Ben Morea, poet Dan Georgakas, and Ron Hahne. Inspired by the actions of the French Situationists and Dutch Provos among others, Black Mask produced a ten issue run newspaper and some broadsheets before going underground. Morea, along with some others involved with Black Mask emerged as the Up Against The Wall Motherfuckers, a name taken from the poem "Black People!" by Amiri Baraka (then still known as LeRoi Jones). In the late 1960s these groups became infamous for actions such as dumping uncollected trash from the Lower East Side in the fountain at Lincoln Center and organizing a protest of The Filmore East through a series of free concerts offered to promote community use of the venue.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Materials documenting influential radical groups of the 1960s. The collection includes a complete run of Black Mask and 61 issues of Rat Subterranean News, 90 flyers and handbills relating to the New York anarchist groups Black Mask and Up Against the Wall Motherfuckers, as well as related affinity groups and others such as the Yippies, ESSO (The East Side Service Organization, or the East Side Survival Organization), and New York Provo.
RELATED MATERIALS
Related collections: #8111, Situationist Collection and #8060, Johan Kugelberg punk collection.
Cite As:
New York Revolutionary Groups collection, #8118. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Radicalism -- United States. Radicalism -- New York (State) -- New York. Radicalism. Social movements. Protest movements. Internationale situationniste. Anarchism -- United States. Anarchism -- New York (State) -- New York. Anarchism. Anarchists. Morea, Ben. Black Mask (Group of artists) Up Against the Wall Motherfuckers (Gang) Youth International Party
